senses.definition: a type of brain teaser or logic puzzle that presents a scenario or problem, challenging individuals to think creatively and critically to find a solution or answer

examples.x: The group played a situation puzzle during the road trip to pass the time.
He introduced a tricky situation puzzle at the party, and everyone was hooked on solving it.
The teacher used a situation puzzle to encourage creative thinking in class.
They spent the evening solving situation puzzles and laughing at each other’s wild guesses.
She enjoys hosting situation puzzles because they spark fun conversations.

senses.definition: a type of problem or game that involves mathematical concepts and requires solving equations, patterns, or logical reasoning to find a solution

examples.x: She loves solving a good mathematical puzzle when she needs a break from work.
I spent the afternoon trying to figure out a tricky mathematical puzzle in my textbook.
My teacher gave us a mathematical puzzle to help us practice problem-solving skills.
He found a mathematical puzzle online and couldn’t stop working on it for hours.
I gave my younger brother a simple mathematical puzzle to keep him entertained during the car ride.

senses.definition: a traditional Japanese geometric puzzle or problem that was historically displayed on wooden tablets called "sangaku" at Shinto shrines and Buddhist temples

examples.x: He spent hours trying to solve the sangaku puzzle that his grandfather had passed down.
The sangaku in the shrine was so complex that only a few mathematicians were able to decipher it.
In our history class, we learned about the tradition of sangaku and how it reflected Japanese interest in mathematics.
The museum had a section dedicated to sangaku, where visitors could see these mathematical puzzles up close.

senses.definition: a type of puzzle that challenges individuals to use deductive reasoning and logical thinking to solve a problem or reach a conclusion

examples.x: The teacher gave us a logic puzzle to solve during class to improve our problem-solving skills.
I spent the whole afternoon trying to solve that tricky logic puzzle in the newspaper.
She loves solving logic puzzles because they challenge her thinking and keep her sharp.
When we went camping, we passed the time by working on a logic puzzle book.
I was stuck on the logic puzzle for hours, but finally, I saw the pattern and figured it out.

senses.definition: a picture on a cardboard that is cut into different pieces and one should fit them together in order for the picture to become whole again

examples.x: She spent the afternoon working on a challenging jigsaw puzzle with her friends.
He felt accomplished when he finally completed the intricate jigsaw puzzle.
The family gathered around the table to start a new jigsaw puzzle for the weekend.
She enjoys the relaxing process of finding the right pieces for her jigsaw puzzle.
He received a beautiful jigsaw puzzle as a birthday gift, featuring a scenic landscape.

senses.definition: a type of puzzle that requires physical manipulation and problem-solving to disassemble, reassemble, or rearrange its components to achieve a specific goal or solution

examples.x: I spent the afternoon trying to solve a mechanical puzzle I found at the store.
He enjoys working on mechanical puzzles to keep his mind sharp.
The mechanical puzzle had so many pieces, I was afraid I might lose one.
After hours of trying, she finally figured out how to solve the mechanical puzzle.
I bought a new mechanical puzzle to keep myself busy during the weekend.

senses.definition: a type of puzzle that involves assembling various pieces to create a specific object or structure

examples.x: The kids spent hours working on the construction puzzle, trying to build the tallest tower possible.
She enjoyed solving the construction puzzle because it challenged her to think carefully about how the pieces fit together.
I found a construction puzzle in the toy store that lets you build cars with different parts.
We worked as a team to complete the construction puzzle, helping each other understand how to arrange the pieces.
The construction puzzle was harder than I expected, but eventually, I figured out how to assemble the pieces.

senses.definition: a 3D combination puzzle with a 3x3x3 grid of smaller cubes, challenging players to solve by aligning each face to have a single color
a type of mechanical puzzle where the objective is to separate or untangle interconnected pieces without using force or cutting

examples.x: I spent an hour trying to solve the disentanglement puzzle, but the pieces just wouldn’t come apart.
The disentanglement puzzle on the table looks simple, but it took me ages to figure out.
He handed me a tricky disentanglement puzzle as a gift, and I couldn’t wait to try it.
She solved the disentanglement puzzle in no time, impressing everyone at the party.
The kids were fascinated by the disentanglement puzzle and kept trying to figure it out.

senses.definition: a type of mechanical puzzle that resembles a small box or container with hidden mechanisms, requiring specific manipulations or movements to open and reveal its contents

examples.x: I received a beautiful puzzle box for my birthday, and I spent hours trying to open it.
The puzzle box on the shelf looks simple, but it took me a while to solve.
After many attempts, he finally managed to open the puzzle box and find the hidden key inside.
My friend loves collecting puzzle boxes; her collection includes some really intricate designs.
We spent the afternoon trying to solve the puzzle box, but it was much harder than we expected.

senses.definition: a type of puzzle that consists of a rectangular board with movable tiles, and the objective is to rearrange the tiles by sliding them into an empty space to form a specific pattern or image

examples.x: I spent hours trying to solve that sliding puzzle, but I just couldn’t get the pieces in the right order.
My little brother loves playing with his sliding puzzle during car rides.
After a few tries, I finally solved the sliding puzzle and felt really proud.
He used his lunch break to unwind by working on a sliding puzzle on his desk.

senses.definition: a traditional Chinese dissection puzzle consisting of seven flat pieces called tans, which are put together to form various shapes and figures

examples.x: My little brother loves playing with his tangram set to make different shapes.
After dinner, we sat around the table and worked on a tangram puzzle together.
At the museum, they had a large tangram puzzle on the wall that visitors could try to solve.
The teacher used a tangram to explain geometric shapes in class.

senses.definition: a puzzle game in which one writes the answers to the clues in numbered boxes

examples.x: She is an expert at solving crosswords in record time.

senses.definition: a word puzzle where players transform one word into another by changing one letter at a time, creating a series of valid English words with each step differing from the previous word by just one letter

examples.x: Word ladders are a fun way to improve your vocabulary.
We played a word ladder game while waiting for the train.
She quickly solved the word ladder, moving from "cat" to "dog" in just three steps.
The teacher gave us a word ladder activity to practice spelling and word formation.

senses.definition: a word game in which one player picks a word and the others attempt to guess it one letter at a time, each time a player fails to guess the word right, the first player draws one part of a hanging man, if the other players fail to guess the word before the drawing is compeleted they lose
a social event held in a bar or pub where teams compete in answering a series of trivia questions on various topics to test their knowledge and win prizes

examples.x: Our team won the pub quiz last month, and we got a free round of drinks as a prize.
I’m terrible at geography, so I usually rely on my friends for that round in the pub quiz.
We always go to the pub quiz on Tuesdays after work; it's become a weekly tradition.
The pub quiz was so competitive last night, we barely managed to come second.

senses.definition: a confusing network of paths separated by bushes or walls, designed in a way that confuses the people who pass through

examples.x: She spent hours trying to find her way out of the maze at the amusement park.
The children ran through the maze, laughing as they tried to find the quickest path.
The maze in the garden was filled with twists and turns, making it hard to navigate.
The maze on the puzzle page was so difficult that it took me a while to finish it.

senses.definition: a number puzzle consisting of nine large squares each divided into nine smaller squares that must be filled with the numbers one to nine, without repeating any of them in the same square, column, or row
a guessing game that involves at least two players in which participants ask a question that has a surprising or clever answer

examples.x: He told a tricky riddle that no one could solve.
The children enjoyed playing a riddle game at the party.
She challenged her friends with a difficult riddle.
They spent hours trying to crack the final riddle.
He solved the riddle after thinking for a long time.

senses.definition: a type of puzzle where the objective is to fill a given area, typically a rectangular grid or a board, with a set of specific shapes without overlapping or leaving any gaps

examples.x: I spent the afternoon working on a tiling puzzle, trying to cover the whole board without any gaps.
The kids love playing with the tiling puzzle because they enjoy figuring out how all the pieces fit together.
The tiling puzzle on the table had a tricky pattern, but I finally managed to solve it after some patience.
She brought out a tiling puzzle during our family game night, and we spent hours trying to complete it.
I find tiling puzzles relaxing; it’s nice to have something to focus on that isn’t too difficult.

senses.definition: a 3D spatial puzzle made up of seven different pieces that can be assembled to form a 3x3x3 cube in various ways, presenting a challenging and creative exercise in spatial reasoning and problem-solving

examples.x: I spent the afternoon trying to solve the Soma cube, but I kept getting stuck.
The Soma cube is a great way to exercise your brain and improve spatial thinking.
My friend introduced me to the Soma cube, and now we compete to see who can solve it the fastest.
He enjoys playing with his Soma cube during his lunch breaks at work.
After a few hours of trial and error, I finally managed to put the Soma cube together.

senses.definition: a type of jigsaw puzzle where pieces have flat edges with specific patterns or shapes that must be matched correctly with adjacent pieces to complete the overall picture or design

examples.x: I spent hours working on the edge-matching puzzle, trying to get all the pieces to fit perfectly.
The kids were excited to play with the edge-matching puzzle, matching the colorful edges together.
She was frustrated because the edge-matching puzzle pieces just wouldn’t align as expected.
We decided to tackle the edge-matching puzzle together as a family activity.
I love doing edge-matching puzzles during rainy days as they help me relax and focus.

senses.definition: a word puzzle or riddle that uses pictures, symbols, or letters to represent words or parts of words, challenging the solver to decipher the hidden meaning or phrase

examples.x: The teacher gave us a rebus to solve during class, and we all had fun trying to figure it out.
I love solving rebus puzzles because they make me think outside the box.
The rebus on the birthday card was a clever way to say "Happy Birthday."
My friend enjoys creating rebus puzzles for me to solve, and she always makes them really tricky.

senses.definition: a game for two players who take a turn to put an X or O in nine provided squares, and the first player who manages to make a row, column, or diagonal of three Xs or Os in those squares wins

examples.x: They played a quick game of tic-tac-toe during their lunch break.
She won the tic-tac-toe game with a clever diagonal move.
He showed his younger sister how to play tic-tac-toe.
The teacher introduced tic-tac-toe as a fun way to practice strategy.
After three rounds of tic-tac-toe, they decided to switch to checkers.

senses.definition: a physical puzzle that involves navigating a small ball or marble through a maze-like structure by tilting or rotating the game board, avoiding holes and obstacles, to reach a specific target or endpoint

examples.x: I bought a small ball maze as a gift for my nephew, and he loves it.
He tilted the ball maze just right to get the ball to the finish line.
We used the ball maze to pass time while waiting for our food at the restaurant.
My little brother is so good at the ball maze; he finishes it in no time.
I remember spending a lot of time with my grandfather, playing with his wooden ball maze.

senses.definition: a type of toy that consists of colored plastic blocks that fit together and using which children can build different things

examples.x: She enjoyed playing with Lego more than any other toy.
The Lego on the floor created a colorful landscape of buildings.
He built a detailed robot using his Lego.
The Lego in the box was designed to create a famous landmark.

senses.definition: a word game where players use a grid of letters to form as many words as possible within a limited time, with words being formed by connecting adjacent letters horizontally, vertically, or diagonally
a puzzle or problem designed to test one's thinking or problem-solving skills

examples.x: She loves solving brain-teasers in the morning to get her mind active.
We had a fun time doing brain-teasers at the party, trying to outsmart each other.
The teacher gave us a brain-teaser to start the class and get us thinking.
If you’re looking for something to exercise your brain, I recommend a good brain-teaser.
The puzzle book contains dozens of tricky brain-teasers.

senses.definition: a type of toy that allows users to build and assemble structures, objects, or models using individual pieces or parts

examples.x: My son loves playing with his construction toy set after school.
I bought a construction toy for my niece's birthday, and she spent hours building with it.
They used a construction toy to create a model of their dream house.
Construction toys are great for helping kids develop their imagination and creativity.
I remember spending weekends as a child playing with construction toys, building everything from cars to castles.

senses.definition: a small, usually rectangular or cubic, building element made from materials like wood, plastic, or foam, used for constructing various structures and fostering creative play and imagination

examples.x: The child spent hours building a castle with her toy blocks.
He loves to arrange the toy blocks in different shapes and patterns.
I found a set of colorful toy blocks in the toy chest.
The teacher gave each student a set of toy blocks for the group activity.
They used the toy blocks to create a bridge that could hold a small toy car.

senses.definition: a pencil-and-paper game played on a grid of dots, where players take turns connecting two dots with a line to create boxes, aiming to complete the most boxes and claim them with their initials to score points

examples.x: We played a few rounds of dots and boxes after dinner last night.
The kids love playing dots and boxes during long car rides.
If you want to play dots and boxes, all you need is a piece of paper and a pen.
Dots and boxes is one of those games that gets more exciting the longer you play.
We passed the time waiting for the bus by playing a quick game of dots and boxes.
